description: Weaknesses in authentication mechanisms that allow attackers to assume other users' identities, compromise passwords, tokens, or exploit implementation flaws.

entries.name: JWT Algorithm Confusion (None/HS256 attack)
entries.description: JWT validation accepts 'none' algorithm or confuses asymmetric (RS256) with symmetric (HS256), allowing token forgery.
entries.how_it_works: If server uses RS256 but accepts HS256, attacker signs token with the PUBLIC key as HMAC secret. If 'none' algorithm accepted, attacker removes signature entirely.
preconditions: JWT library doesn't enforce expected algorithm
entries.detection_guidance: Check JWT validation code: does it specify algorithms=['RS256']? Or does it accept whatever the token header says?
exploit_payloads: Header: {"alg": "HS256"} + sign with RS256 public key as HMAC secret
entries.vulnerable_code: payload = jwt.decode(token, public_key)  # No algorithms specified
remediation: Always specify algorithms parameter in JWT decode
Never accept 'none' algorithm
Use asymmetric algorithms (RS256/ES256) for distributed systems

entries.name: JWT Claim Manipulation (Mutable Custom Attributes)
entries.description: User can modify their own JWT claims (e.g., role, tenant_id) via identity provider API because custom attributes are mutable.
entries.how_it_works: Cognito/Auth0 custom attributes marked as 'mutable' can be changed by the user via UpdateUserAttributes API. Next token issued contains the modified claim. If the application trusts these claims for authorization, privilege escalation occurs.
preconditions: Application trusts JWT claims for authorization decisions
No server-side validation of claim values against a policy store
affected_components: AWS Cognito with mutable custom attributes
Any IdP allowing user self-modification of claims
entries.detection_guidance: Check Cognito UserPool definition: are custom:role or custom:tenant_id attributes mutable=True? Check if authorizer trusts these without additional verification.
exploit_payloads: aws cognito-idp update-user-attributes --user-pool-id POOL_ID --access-token TOKEN --user-attributes Name=custom:role,Value=admin
aws cognito-idp update-user-attributes --user-pool-id POOL_ID --access-token TOKEN --user-attributes Name=custom:tenant_id,Value=TARGET_TENANT
entries.vulnerable_code:
# CDK: custom attributes mutable
cognito.StringAttribute(mutable=True)  # role attribute

# Authorizer trusts claim directly
role = jwt_claims['custom:role']  # User can set this to 'admin'
entries.safe_code:
# CDK: immutable custom attributes
cognito.StringAttribute(mutable=False)

# Or: verify role against policy store
role = jwt_claims['custom:role']
db_role = policies_table.get_item(Key={'pk': f'USER#{user_id}'})['Item']['role']
assert role == db_role, 'Role mismatch'
remediation: Set security-critical attributes to mutable=False
Use a pre-token-generation trigger to inject claims from a trusted source
Verify claims against a policy store on every request
Use groups instead of custom attributes for role management

entries.name: Session Fixation / No Token Rotation
entries.description: Refresh tokens not rotated on use, allowing persistent access with a stolen token.
entries.how_it_works: If a refresh token is stolen (via XSS, log exposure, or device theft), attacker can generate new access tokens indefinitely until the refresh token expires (30 days). Without rotation, there's no way to detect or revoke the stolen token.
preconditions: No device binding or anomaly detection
impact: Persistent unauthorized access for refresh token lifetime
entries.detection_guidance: Check Cognito configuration: is refresh token rotation enabled? Check refresh token validity period. Check if global_sign_out is available for emergency revocation.
exploit_payloads: Steal refresh token → Use it from different IP/device → Generate unlimited access tokens for 30 days
entries.vulnerable_code:
# Cognito config: no rotation
auth_flows=['ALLOW_REFRESH_TOKEN_AUTH']
# Validity: 30 days, no rotation
entries.safe_code:
# Enable rotation: each refresh returns a new refresh token
# Old refresh token is invalidated
# Detect: if old token is used after rotation → revoke all tokens (replay detection)
remediation: Enable refresh token rotation
Reduce refresh token validity (7 days instead of 30)
Implement device binding
Add anomaly detection (new IP/device)

entries.name: Brute Force / Credential Stuffing
entries.description: Authentication endpoint allows unlimited login attempts without rate limiting or account lockout.
entries.how_it_works: Attacker uses lists of leaked credentials (credential stuffing) or systematic password guessing (brute force) against the login endpoint.
preconditions: No account lockout after failed attempts
entries.detection_guidance: Check: does the login endpoint have rate limiting? Is Cognito advanced security (CompromisedCredentialsRiskConfiguration) enabled? Are failed attempts logged and alerted on?
exploit_payloads: Automated credential stuffing with Hydra/Burp Intruder against /auth/login endpoint
entries.vulnerable_code:
# API Gateway: 100 req/sec throttle (shared across all users)
# No per-account lockout
# No CAPTCHA
entries.safe_code:
# Cognito advanced security features
user_pool.add_trigger_operation(
    cognito.UserPoolOperation.PRE_AUTHENTICATION,
    risk_based_lockout_lambda
)
remediation: Enable Cognito advanced security features
Implement account lockout after N failures
Add CAPTCHA after 3 failures
Rate limit per IP AND per account

entries.name: Insufficient Session Expiration
entries.description: Sessions remain valid too long, increasing the window for token theft exploitation.
entries.how_it_works: Long-lived tokens (access: 1hr, refresh: 30 days) provide extended attack windows. If stolen, attacker has prolonged access.
impact: Extended window for exploiting stolen tokens
entries.detection_guidance: Check token validity settings: AccessTokenValidity, IdTokenValidity, RefreshTokenValidity in Cognito UserPoolClient.
entries.vulnerable_code:
# Access: 1 hour, Refresh: 30 days
RefreshTokenValidity=30  # days
entries.safe_code:
# Access: 15 minutes, Refresh: 7 days with rotation
AccessTokenValidity=15  # minutes
RefreshTokenValidity=7  # days
# Plus: rotation enabled
remediation: Reduce token validity to minimum acceptable
Implement refresh token rotation
Add idle timeout (revoke after N minutes inactive)

entries.name: Insufficiently Protected Credentials
entries.description: Credentials (tokens, passwords) stored in accessible locations or transmitted insecurely.
entries.how_it_works: Tokens stored in localStorage (persistent, accessible to XSS) or logged to monitoring systems. Passwords transmitted without TLS.
preconditions: HTTP (not HTTPS) used for auth endpoints
entries.detection_guidance: Check frontend: localStorage vs sessionStorage for tokens. Check backend: are tokens/passwords ever logged? Check: is HTTPS enforced?
remediation: Use sessionStorage or HttpOnly cookies
Never log credentials or tokens
Enforce HTTPS on all endpoints
Use structured logging with field filtering

entries.name: Authentication Bypass via API Key Shared Across Users
entries.description: Single API key shared across all users provides no per-user authentication or attribution.
entries.how_it_works: API key embedded in frontend is identical for all users. Attacker extracts it and makes API calls without the application's UI, bypassing any client-side controls.
preconditions: API key visible in client-side code
Same key used for all users
Backend relies on API key as authentication (rather than just throttling)
impact: No per-user attribution of API calls
Shared rate limit means one abuser affects all users
entries.detection_guidance: Search frontend code for API key values or X-Api-Key headers. Check if the same key is used by all users.
entries.vulnerable_code:
// Frontend: shared API key
fetch(url, { headers: { 'X-Api-Key': 'shared-key-for-all-users' } })
remediation: Use per-user tokens (Cognito) as primary authentication
API keys for rate limiting only, not authentication
If API keys needed: issue per-user or per-tenant keys
